name: 2 girls 1 camp
description: 2 girls 1 camp is one of the silliest camps on playa! During the day time we're slinging slushies - The No.1 (Yellow) and The No.2 (Brown). Fiber-spiked for flow, booze-spiked for fun. Our signature event- traditional coconut oil wrestling at the Center Camp Oculus.
duties:
"Campers typically:
* Work a slushie shift
* Support one wrestling event
* Take one kitchen crew day
* Participate in strike"
cities: NYC, SF Bay Area

name: A Shack of Sit
description: Where sit happens! Come party with the smarties at Burning Man's only Mensa-affiliated camp. We offer a rest stop for wayward burners with water, electrolytes, shade, and activities. A Shack of Sit has excellent placement this year and we have room for more campers.
duties: Our campers have shifts maintaining the Shack, preparing meals, and running events. Everyone that is able helps during tear down.
dues: $500-600, which goes to food, water, grey water, trailer storage, logistics, and infrastructure improvements.
cities: We are from all over, our leadership is in Los Angeles, Fort Collins CO, and Rochester NY.

name: AAA Astro accessible arcade
description: AAA Astro is an accessible arcade camp from the Dawn Collective in San Jose. The camp communalizes all major infrastructure and asks campers to assist with arcade games.
duties: Assist with arcade games
dues: $750 camp infrastructure
cities: San Jose

name: AdramaNation
description: AdramaNation is a diverse camp focusing on a drama free, meaningful, interactive and fun- filled burn. We are an old-school , easy going camp that gifts delicious delicacies, cheeky cocktails, mocktails, and a hydration station. Our drama dump offers a safe space for people to leave their drama and to relax in a soft, pillow laden space.
duties: We believe in the ten principles and expect our campmates to as well.  All campmates are required to strike camp.  Every camp mate is required to take one breakfast shift in addition to two additional hours of either cooking or bar interactivity during the week.  Everyone looks for moop daily.  We aren't your mom, so you will need to clean up after yourself.  Be nice or leave!  We are a no drama zone.
dues: $310 - kitchen, shade, shower, art car
cities: New Orleans, Seattle, Dallas, Tiajuana, Orlando, Tahoe

name: Alternative Energy Zone “AEZ”
description: We are big on helping you burn without a generator. We can help you design a solar system. You can have your own space. Easy in and out.
duties: Leave no trace. No dues required. We do have offerings you can volunteer at, but not required.
dues: Donations are welcome. It goes toward infrastructure. We feel like, if it’s your first time with us, see if you like it. If you feel inspired by the AEZ, then consider a donation next year.
cities: Reno, Phoenix, Sacramento, Bay Area, Ashland, Portland, Seattle, Berlin

name: Amazone Camp
description: We are the first Brazilian Camp at Burning Man. We brought the first 3 Brazilian art pieces and produced the first Brazilian doc about the event. today we are a multicultural camp, with a population coming form over 15 countries. We take the Burner Culture very seriously.
duties: Our camp is organized in 14 different ares (or "departments"). Population get involved in structural activites (power grid, water management, LNT management, build, strike, cooking) and also in more social (hosting, organizing ceremonies, signage, serving pizza and tending our bar)
cities: Porto Alegre, Belo Horizonte, Ro de Janeiro, São Paulo, Oakland, San Francisco, Los Angeles, New York, Las Vegas, Paris, Genebra, Brussels

name: And Then There's Only LOVE (Orgy Dome)
description: And Then There's Only LOVE is home to the world famous Orgy Dome. We provide a safer, consent-oriented and air conditioned space to explore adult-themed play. We have a strong camper culture and daily camper events for interacting socially. We celebrate and encourage diversity in all forms.
duties: Three 3-4 hour shifts in camp and the Dome, help with strike.
dues: $500 - toward communal power, water for kitchen and shower, grey water pumping, shade, kitchen, and food during build and strike
cities: Las Vegas, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Portland, Austin

name: Anonymous Village
description: Anonymous Village provides a supportive community and safe camping experience for people in recovery from drugs, alcohol, or other addiction. The camp hosts 12-step meetings and other clean and sober events, funded by voluntary contributions with no required dues.
duties: Build, strike, moop, participate or lead 12-step meetings
dues: We are funded by voluntary contributions, no dues.
cities: Bay Area and Reno

name: Apres Ski Injury
description: Après Ski Injury is an international, high-energy mountain-bar dropped into the dust: part ski lodge, part dance floor, part bad downhill idea with excellent execution. In BRC we serve après-ski drinks, run our ski ramp, host ski races, DJs, bar games, and a photo corner for participants who want proof that this really happened. We are a mixed crew from across the world, united by hospitality, playful chaos, and the belief that Burning Man deserves more ski boots, more music, and fewer sensible decisions.
duties: Two bar shifts a 4h (including ice), helping tear down and clean up, a mature respectful attitude towards other camp members, a camp fee that solely goes into the camps offerings
dues: 500 USD. Everyone pays, no exception. It goes into drinks, infrastructure and annual costs such as container storage. No one is earning a single cent.
cities: SLO, Boston, Queretaro (Mex), Gex (Fra)

name: Astral Lab
description: Astral Lab welcomes all to our community of music loving, dust stomping, hot-ass weirdos. Prepare for active meditation through dance! Astral Project with us on the playa in 2026
duties: Help out in either the Bar, Kitchen, or M.O.O.P. Crews
dues: $500. This is the shared cost of brining the camp ammenities and community offering to build our piece of the city.
cities: San Fransisco USA,  Los Angeles USA, Paris France, Berlin Germany, Sao Paulo Brazil

name: Astro Cats
description: We are a medium-size camp with members from different parts of the US and the world. We treasure the diversity of our backgrounds, cultures, and talents. Our main activities this year are aerial performances, ramen, sushi and sake....
duties: 2 camp shifts of 3 hours and 1 ice run.
dues: Camp Cost Share is US $150.00. It covers transportation of camp infrastructure, drinking water, grey water disposal, open kitchen,  alchole and sake for camp events.
cities: San Francisco, Bay Area

name: Awesomesauce
description: Camp Awesomesauce is a 21-100+ aged, queer-affirming sparkle of lovely unicorns formed in 2014 with the goal of keeping our playa family together. We are small enough so that everyone knows your name, and big enough to pool our magic and contribute meaningfully to both our camp experience and Black Rock City.
duties:
"All campmates are required to help with build (before the burn) or take down (after the burn)
Commitment to volunteer for three shifts during the week:
- Morning coffee (occasionally with a side of questionable advice)
- Afternoon UnStable Bar, Glamstation or other events
- Family Dinner"
dues: $350 They go towards our camp container and the costs of running the camp
cities: San Francisco, New York, Moab, Reno

name: Barbie Death Village
description: Barbie Death Village is comprised of people of all ages, interests, beliefs, orientations... all are welcome. This is one of the things that makes us... us!
duties: Camp members are required to have three work shifts throughout the week and build week.
dues: Camp dues is $200. It goes towards infrastructure, stocking the bar, maintenance and car of the Mutant Vehicle (the Barbie Whorehouse), swag, maintenance of the shower, maintaining trailers for transport, storage, yearly art support gift, etc.
cities: Reno, NV

name: Better Together
description: Better Together Camp is where sparks fly and connections are made, literally, we’re not responsible for any Playa-induced lightning strikes. Our Speed Dating isn’t your average meet-and-greet; it’s all about real conversations, radical self-expression, and a little bit of magic. Whether you’re a Burning Man newbie or a dusty veteran, we’ve got a spot for you to connect, laugh, and maybe even find your Playa soulmate (or just a good chat). In our camp, everyone’s welcome, and everyone’s encouraged to be their weird, wonderful selves.
duties:
"- shift at least in one event
- shift in the kitchen (breakfast)
- shift LNT
- build/strike"
dues: 1000$ (camp infrastructure and logistics; open bar and events for guests; water/wc management; breakfasts)
cities: Zurich, Los Angeles, San Francisco, New York, Tel Aviv

name: Bioluminati (Home of the Bike Mutation Station)
description: Our camp is a close knit community (this year we are about 15 campers) and our focus is decorating bikes. We have great infrastructure, low dues and we want campers who are motivated to work hard and play hard. We need help on set up and break down and with bike mutation station shifts. We provide at least one meal a day
duties: We need help setting up and taking down infrastructure,  bike mutation shifts and meal shifts.
dues: $300 - towards infrastucture, meals and supplies
cities: Grass Valley, Bay Area, Atlanta

name: BLACK ROCK ADVENTURES BUS STOP!
description: We are a small camp of about 15 people - we are an art support camp with a twist. We have an artwork BUS STOP! (pic on website) that we service with 9am accessible tours on our ""bus"" - it's a rustic adventure rig and is also pictured on our website. We also go out at night, and for the big burns but, pending more sober-when-they-drive-drivers we offer night tours only by word of internet prior to the burn and word of mouth at the Burn, we look to provide mobility support to the Black Rock community and can take wheelchairs but mostly service Burners who have a hard time getting around on two feet as well as the able bodied.
duties: 4-6 hours volunteer time, help break camp. Volunteer time means either driving or spotting on our adventure rig during our accessible tours.
dues: $185 per person. Helps offset costs for storage, gas, maintenance of the BUS STOP! and the adventure rig. Helps to offset costs for trailing stuff, kitchen build out, shade structure build out, thanks in advance.
cities: Los Angeles CA, Billings MT

name: Black Rock City Passport Office
description: We are a 30-40ish person camp with a mix of playa veterans and first timers. Our gift is a realistic, personalized Black Rock City passport, complete with an on-playa photo, and a directory of 150+ partner camps, each contributing their own stamp. Every passport is an invitation to wander, stumble on new camps you'd never have found otherwise, and meet people you may have never met. It's a call to adventure!
duties: Each camp member is expected to work two 3.5-hour shifts distributing passports and greeting visitors. Every camper is also expected to participate in camp strike, which occurs Saturday before the Man burns.
dues: Camp dues are $450-500 depending on final camp count. The bulk of the dues goes toward printing passports. The remainder contribute to camp infrastructure (camp shade, kitchen, off playa storage, drinking water, gray water disposal, and 2 camp meals).
cities: We're from all over! San Francisco Bay Area, Oregon, Colorado, Texas, New York, New Jersey, and some international.

name: Black Rock City Shipyard
description: A dusty shipyard oasis fueled by sailors, hot dogs, and strong cocktails.
duties: 2 shifts
cities: Miami/ NY

name: Black Rock Public Library
description: We are a small camp that brings a library to Black Rock City. We love having first-time burners in our camp.
duties: We expect you to do a shift as a librarian - shelving booking and helping patrons check out books.   (Also expect help with tear down at the end of the week)
dues: Dues are $300.  They go toward water, camp infrastructure, books.
cities: Bay Area, Minneapolis, Washington D.C.

name: Blazing Death Ship
description: The Blazing Death Ship was created in 2013 when a bunch of fairly inexperienced burners got struck by inspiration and decided to build their own mutant vehicle. Having absolutely no relevant prior experience, they took welding classes and learned the ropes of metal fabrication and design, resulting in hours (read: an entire summer) spent on metal scrap yards, now defunct makers spaces, and backyards of friends who were told that this wouldn’t take over their entire garden for that long… The rest was learned in the welding school of hard knocks which resulted in burning of some clothes and lacerating some legs with an angle grinder and having to go to an emergency room… But all of these valuable learning experiences led to creating the coolest viking-themed Mutant Vehicle on playa, and the foundation for a mutant vehicle support camp that has been returning every year since.
duties:
"You are responsible for cooking dinner one night for the entire camp. If this is something you absolutely don’t want or can’t do, please let us know and we figure something else out. 
You have to sign up for 3 other shifts: Cleaning after mealtime, making breakfast, driving the ship, ice runs, bartending, meat hall tending … We'll be sharing a sign up sheet pre burn for you to decide!"
dues: Our base level minimum is $450. We understand that everyone's financial situation is different, so we operate on a sliding scale pricing model. If you're able to contribute a bit more (for example, $550 or $5,000), it would greatly help us recover from a deficit of over $6,000 from last year. Think of it as a way to collectively support the camp and ensure we can keep bringing the Blazing Death Ship to life! We release our transparent finances to camp members after each burn so you can see what your money contributed to. And if you are in a situation of financial hardship with difficulty affording this, please reach out and we’ll try to figure something out - we don’t want money to be the reason you can’t join.
cities: California, New York, New Orleans, Texas, Florida, all over the place!

name: BRC Snow Club
description: We bring a dry ski slope, equipment ""rental"" shop, and après vibes to playa! Our HQ is in the San Francisco Bay Area, but we're also a year-round global community of Burners who love snow sports. We hold true to the 10 principles and are NOT a camp-as-a-service, but we do have all the amenities. Co-led by Major Trouble (the unofficial ""weatherman"" in 2025) and MORE!
duties: Our Camper Agreements correlate directly to the 10 Principles + Consent + Flexibility
dues: See Camp Info Deck: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1lvppQ-cTRvGAM88l1nlQN5XAT6w76GBTYVT4xdmKAxc
cities: SF Bay Area, Tahoe, and all over!

name: BRC Studio & Spa
description: We offer massage classes and sessions, as well as fine Art Nude photography workshops and sessions
duties: Current or ongoing massage therapists are most welcome, but if you have an interest we will teach you some life-long skills! Also need help signing people up for workshops and sessions. Build and strike might be helpful too.
dues: $150 upwards, depends on services. Reduced for experienced masseuses
cities: Vegas, Miami

name: BUBBLE LOUNGE
description: Founded in 1999, the Bubble Lounge is one of the longest-running theme camps at Burning Man. Produced for over twenty years by its co-founder, Esquire Jauchem, the Bubble Lounge has always been a welcoming, inclusive, cool space with a chill vibe. And this year it will celebrate 27 years at Black Rock City on the 7.30 and B Plaza! Rising from the dust storms of last year.
duties: Participation, and dont be a dick. We ask each camper to volunteer for a minimum of 1x 4hr shift - that's all! Anyone who wants to be awesome can also apply for early entry to help us build (we have a couple of build slots available)
dues: $450, with flat rates for RVs
cities: Gerlach (yeah, for real!), and Los Angeles - we have always been a well traveled camp and house many international burners including German and UK people.

name: Camp Contact
description: Camp Contact is all about Embodiment Practices- Contact Improv, Acro Yoga, Ecstatic Dance & Authentic Relating are our pillars, and we also love yoga, meditation, breathwork, aerial arts, tantra, bodywork and beyond. We also have a side interest around Metamodern Spirituality, Game~B, The Liminal Web and addressing the metacrisis.
duties: We need your help with Strike on Saturday, Sunday, Monday and Tuesday morning
dues: Dues range from $675 to $1025 and go towards Shade, Kitchen & Meals, Beverages, Water for Drinking, Cleaning & Showering, Dance Space, Chill & Event Dome, Fuel, SteamBath, Power, Tools, Bikes, Satellite Internet, Tents, Lighting, Greywater Disposal, Trash Disposal, Composting, etc
cities: Washington, DC, Quebec City, New York City, Bay Area, Berlin, Miami,  Seattle,  Australia, Israel, Laramie, Bentonville, Arkansas

name: CAMP JUICY
description: Afternoon fresh fruit smoothies and DJs. A@445. We are a do it yourself camp without amenities.
duties: 4 work shifts for the week. Either smoothies or teardown (Saturday) Must leave by Mon Sept 7 at 8AM
dues: $375 or $750 sponsor
cities: Too many but we are based in Los Angeles

name: Camp Just Ahead
description: This will be the camp's 4th year, we took off 2 years and miss the dust! We keep our camp simple, offering communal shade (people, not tents), kitchenette, some power, some Internet. This keeps the burn affordable, and allows us to focus our time on our interactivity (our hilarious Wheel of Immediacy), our volunteer shifts and personal gifting, and enjoying the burn! We're a great place for new burners who are looking for a place to call home, not a hotel to check into as one more anonymous number.
duties:
"We ask for each camper to help with three 2-hour shifts for our interactivity! Basically, meeting people, explaining our Wheel of Immediacy, encouraging and rooting for them when they choose an activity, maybe joining in with them if you want! It's a lot of fun. You can 

We also expect anyone who is on-playa on Sunday morning to help with Strike, so we can roll off the playa either before Temple Burn (if everyone wants to) or immediately after.

We could use more folks for our Saturday (1 day early) build! But that is fully optional.

Everyone is expected to keep shared spaces clean and MOOP-free and to follow all LNT guidance (including grey water minimization/management)."
dues: No dues this year!
cities: Beaumont, TX; San Antonio, TX; Austin, TX; Bay Area, CA

name: Camp Slow Your Roll
description: Inspired by the sloth, our camp encourages BRC to slow down, enjoy the burn, and connect with the awesome dusty jungle out there. Our interactivity is all things sloooooooooow - The slowest bike race, slow service at our lounge (the price of a drink is the time you have to wait to get it), slow line dance lessons, and slow drunk yoga. We are small, about 10-15 people. Our gift to the playa is primarily our interactivity to the broader community, thus practicing self-reliance inside the camp - we provide only a few amenities: ice, a kitchenette with a microwave and kettle, and solar power grid to power/charge small devices/e-bikes.
duties: We require two 1.5-2 hr shifts to run our interactivity (M-Th), as well as one ice run for the camp and one kitchenette cleaning shift (5 min).
dues: Approx $400 (Exact dues TBD). This pays for consumables and building+storing our interactivity and communal camp spaces/power, plus personal ice.
cities: San Francisco Area, France

name: Camp THREAT
description: 24/7 Communist Dive Bar and EDM-free community center.
duties: Build/Strike and/or sign up for shift through burn week; kitchen, MOOP, Bartending
dues: $700 - logtistics and trailer storage/transport, generator rental, fuel, food, propane flame effects, bar supplies
cities: Portland, OR, Salt Lake City

name: Candyland
description: Small camp last 15 years. Started by Ranger and Earth Guardian volunters. We gift visitors with canndy from old vending machines, and invite adults to play our Claw machine to win sex toys.
duties: Help set up and take down camp. Have fun interacting with visitors
cities: Steamboat Springs. Portland,  and Tucson

name: Casa de la Rumba
description: Rumberos, degenerates, smooth talkers, fake millionaires, and anyone who enjoys being a child: **Casa de la Rumba is open for applications!!** As every year, we’re bringing the full **1920s prohibition-era casino** fantasy to the playa. Think smoky backroom energy, fake money, suspiciously charming dealers, dices, sinning wheels and decks flowing over dramatic accusations, ridiculous gambling rituals, and the chance to spend your night pretending you’re in the 20s while lovingly messing with strangers in the most Burning Man way possible. We’ve also got a killer placement this year: **7:30 & E, on an avenue and a corner**, which means the Casino is going to be in a prime spot for maximum chaos, connection, and nonsense. If your idea of fun is dressing like you’re in the 20s, staying fully in character, messing with people, inventing fake drama, and creating an immersive experience for strangers, this is very much your camp.
duties: Cooking 1 day and doing their shifts
dues: $550. Camp contribution includes daily dinner, a covered kitchen, bedouin common area, shaded living quarters, fresh/grey water management, daily ice for camp use, fans, workshops, activities, and our beloved Casino.
cities: San Francisco, Madrid, LA, Barcelona, London

name: Clevian Circularity
description: Mainly Wisconsin-based Camp for 25 years now. We specialize in direct, personal, small-scale interactivities, which we run in 2-hour shifts all day
duties: Be self-sufficient. Think up something creative for your Interactivity shifts, which we do in teams of two. An enclosed 10 X 20 shade tent is provided, with some tables, chairs, and lighting you can use.
dues: None.
cities: Milwaukee, Madison, Appleton

name: Cognitive Research Institute
description: The paperwork is real. The science is not. The Cognitive Research Institute is a deadpan Soviet-era memory research facility (est. 1973) opening its Black Rock field station for a two-season run — assessment stations, five diagnosable conditions, a machine that destroys surrendered memories, and a bar called the Memory Hole to recover in.
duties:
"We need people who want to run the experience: wear the lab coat, hold the deadpan, enjoy the incredulous stares, etc, along with ice runs and keeping the kitchen tidy.

If you'd like to participate in the camp food plan, contribute 20 meals in vacusealed bags and gain access to the canp's chest freezer with everyone's contributions."
dues: 500 - storage container rental, fresh and grey water delivery, shade structure maintenance, materials for the memory destruction unit and hosted events
cities: SF/Oakland, Santa Cruz, and Sacramento

name: Comfort & Joy
description: Comfort & Joy is one of the longest-running queer camps on playa - a colorful, deeply participatory home for people who believe Burning Man can be more than a party. We come to Black Rock City to help build a world where freedom is real, self-expression is sacred, and comfort, pleasure, play, and belonging are not luxuries, but something everyone deserves to share. This is not a spectator camp. It is a village we build together.
duties: Volunteer shifts: setup, strike and in-week: 4-6 hours total
dues: 800 - Infrastructure, Food, Water
cities: San Francisco & International

name: Dark Heart
description: Dark Heart is a community of spiritual creators anchored by technical entrepreneurs, healing arts practitioners, and flow and media artists. We collaborate and amplify each other while dreaming and creating a better world for the greatest good of all. Dark Heart follows the transformative path of fire, ritualized through fire rituals and embodied through fire art and fire dance, to burn away old ways of thinking and being that no longer serve and to welcome in new energies and vistas that do. Dark Heart works primarily in the energy of the east, of the hero's return journey, collapsing the duality of doing and being to the flow state where we embody both simultaneously, where we can walk in both worlds with awareness and full engagement, where we can have an impact that is aligned with the greatest good.
duties: Build and/or strike, plus two 2-hour shifts during the week to keep things running
dues: $285, for gear storage/delivery, shade, kitchen, etc; more elaborate amenities available from our HUB partner for additional cost
cities: Laramie, Denver, San Francisco, Hawaii

name: Dios del Tiempo
description: We are an art support camp. The philosophy of our piece revolves around our relationship with time — how we move through it, how it moves through us, and how rarely we truly inhabit it. The God of Time remains seated, watching the sun from sunrise to sunset. He marks the clock's time by pointing its hand to a giant sundial.
duties: We are looking to fill different roles, so the duties vary (building the installation/cooking at camp/receiving shipments before BM etc). You can find more details at our website.
dues: Depending on the level of involvement, up to $900. The dues go toward the installation and the common infrastructure.
cities: Moscow, Tel Aviv

name: Diptown
description: Diptown is an international art support camp made of artists, builders, engineers, immigrants, and long-time Burners who love creating strange, generous, and slightly impossible things in the desert. We have brought large-scale public art to BRC, including Diptown, Desert Splash, and other collaborative projects, and our camp exists to support art builds, mutant vehicles, and weird immersive experiences. We also host BARBORIS, our tiny “vodka oasis” — a cold-shot, warm-heart bar where dusty travelers can restore their body, soul, and sense of absurdity.
duties: All camp members are expected to participate, not just consume. Everyone helps with setup, strike, LNT/MOOP, shared kitchen duties, water/gray water/trash systems, and keeping the camp safe and functional. Members also take shifts for our BARBORIS bar/events and help support our art projects when needed. We expect people to be kind, reliable, sober-enough when responsible for safety tasks, and respectful of BRC culture
cities: Our campmates are mostly located in Reno/Sparks, San Francisco Bay Area, Los Angeles, Seattle, New York, Lisbon, Berlin, Amsterdam, Tbilisi, and other cities in the US and Europe. We are also happy to meet new campers by video call if they are not near one of our people.

name: DragonCamp Armory
description: We run workshops to teach women how to make their own metal bikini armor costume. ( think Game of Thrones, House of the Dragon, sword and sorcery/sci-fi movies. Women select a pattern, cut and hand hammer the aluminum into a cool comfortable sexy costume to wear and share on the playa. we assist and supervise activity.
duties: Help with workshop activities 4-5 mornings only, help set-up, tear down camp workshop. We have space but provide no facilities or amenities.
dues: no dues
cities: Orange County, California

name: dust & stars
description: at dust & stars, we take cults very, very unseriously. for many years, we’ve donned black robes and held initiation in our maximalist temple. this year, we’ll feature a different cult each day, from Midsommar to MLMs, with themed drinks, gifts, activities, & mostly gentle brainwashing. WE WELCOME ALL WHO DARE
duties: at least three 2-hour "culty hour" shifts (pure fun!); bringing/cooking one family dinner all week; and helping with strike. available to build? even better! when it comes to costume, satirical cult comedy, & creativity, we love folks who wanna jump right in.
dues: we've adopted a sliding scale/pay what you can system, averaging $350-450. as a smaller camp, dues primarily go to infrastrucure storage & transportation, along with any replacement materials & other necessary communal/interactivity items. budget is shared with campmates.
cities: leads are in los angeles, but our diaspora is global! where are you? (why are you?)

name: DUSTFISH
description: We are radically inclusive, welcome queer and bipoc concepts, serve pho all week night and day along with libations and great music. Camp members have access to camp showers, toilets and a great location in front of the art.
duties:
"8 hours of volunteer during the week, 
4 hours of moop begore departure"
dues: $450 additional charge for electricity
cities: Bay Area, Las Vegas

name: Dusty Dreamers
description: We are a European camp with freedom and celebration at our core
duties: Help with build (arrive Friday Aug 28), and one dinner shift and one activity (3hrs total work) during the event
dues: $700 and includes a bike, tent, food, water and everything else
cities: Paris, Stockholm, Malaga

name: Dusty Mule Saloon and Playground
description: Our camp is an easy-going family that focuses on belly laughs for our guests. We are communal and have family dinner each night, lovingly made by and for each other. We connect well before Playa, so we are connected in camp.
duties: 2 shifts and participation in creating one night's dinner
dues: 300 - storage and bar supplies
cities: Denver, Bay area, Amsterdam, tel aviv, Bali, Turkey

name: Erotic Inclination Photo Studio
description: We’re a boutique-sized camp (15-20 people)—a tight-knit crew building this experience from the ground up. Our primary gift to the playa is hosting personal photo shoots that celebrate body positivity and radical self-expression. Camp Experience: 3 meals a day, an open bar (booze and NA beverages), a massive 100ftx50ft shade structure, an authentic Moroccan Bedouin tent lounge, solar power, and a 5,000-watt sound system. Events hosted at BM2026: - sensual spanking workshops - sound healing - tantric love workshop - poetry on-the-go - dance parties and guest DJs throughout the week
duties:
"Your duties may include (but not limited to):
1. Photography Assistant & Model Coordinator
Provide direct, on-site support to our resident photographer during active portrait and art sessions
Assist with model staging, lighting adjustments, wardrobe prep, and ensuring participants feel comfortable and confident during their sessions
2. Studio Concierge & Crowd Management
Act as the welcoming face of the studio, manage the public queue, greet walk-in participants, and regulate the crowd flow. This role is vital to maintaining an organized, stress-free environment so the photographer can focus entirely on their craft
3. Cross-Functional Camp Logistics & Event Support
Provide flexible, hands-on support across various camp infrastructure projects based on your personal skill sets
Opportunities include live DJing/audio mixing, small-scale event setup, bar tending, and kitchen logistics. We actively cross-train members and will match your unique talents to the right team project"
dues: Camp dues are set at $1,000 per person and are allocated across the following essential categories: Sustenance & Hospitality: Bulk procurement of fresh ingredients for three daily communal meals, potable water, ice, and open-bar inventory (alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages). Logistics & Transport: Box truck rental, transport fuel, and transit logistics required to haul heavy camp infrastructure to and from the playa. Operational Consumables: Fuel, propane for cooking, and utility items required to run the camp grid and kitchen. Year-Round Maintenance: Off-playa annual storage unit fees, equipment repairs, structural maintenance, and necessary upgrades to the solar array and 100x50 shade canopy.
cities: SF Bay Area (CA), Singapore, Kona (HI), Vilnius (Lithuania), Edmonton (Canada)

name: Fantastica Music Healing Camp
description: Reset with us in a space where the magical sounds of live guitar gently guide you into relaxation and inner balance. Here, you can pause, release tension, and simply be. Our camp creates an atmosphere of calm, comfort, and mindful presence. Open to all who seek restoration and a deeper connection with themselves.
duties: Each camp member helps and becomes part of our team. We need help with the kitchen, cleaning, the Sound Healing tent, electric bike charging, and other small tasks. We expect our members to be responsible, friendly, and ready to help each other. ♥️
dues: Camp dues are $700  per person and go toward food, water, electricity, and camp tent and infrastructure.
cities: Los Angeles

name: Friendlandia
description: We're a small, personal camp centered around the celebration of friendships. We offer self guided Friendgagement ceremonies and friendship bracelet making workshops.
duties: Minimum attending setup (8/28-8/29) or strike (9/6), preferably both. Make, serve and clean up 1 dinner for the camp, co-host 1-2 friendship bracelet making workshops (3hrs ea), 2-3 small miscellaneous chores ex ice run or gray water management.
dues: $600-$700.  Essentially includes everything but your ticket, vp, tent/bedding and clothes.  Dues cover camp infrastructure maintenance, storage and transportation. Food, water, ice, nominal power (solar), shower, grey water, full kitchen, a bike to borrow
cities: Bay Area, Reno, Tucson

name: Fucking Joe Camp
description: We’re Fucking Joe Camp, a small but mighty camp of around 15-20, headquartered in San Jose with campers hailing from Texas, Arizona, Belgium, Ecuador, Poland, Hawaii, and beyond. Reflecting the diversity of our crew, our interactivity is just as vibrant and varied: Tea and Tarot, Pole Dancing Lessons and Competitions, Speedfriending, Bloody Twisted, and our Sensual Sensory Experience. And of course, our mostly-always-on, illuminated pole dancing platform invites spontaneous performances day and night. We’re all about fun, games, dancing, deep conversations, and genuine connection.
duties: Contribute! We're small so need everyone's help to make magic! Help build, strike, and participate in most of our fun events. :) It's a lot of work but worth it!
dues: Yes, $650. Water, food, shade, shower, gray water removal.
cities: San Jose, CA; Austin, TX; Phoenix, AZ; Ft. Worth, TX; Bocas, Panama; Los Angeles, CA; Kona, HI

name: Galactic Cruzaders
description: Theme Camp, Cacophony Society, DJ events, amazing food and music, primarily an international camp. Contact Playa Dad for camps "welcome" email that explains more.
duties: Combination of build-week and striking of camp, only with duties during the week.  All members take in a small portion of the work load.  Shifts are short, typically only 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Unless you have taken on Lead position.  Playa Dad wants members not tied to camp, but exploring Black Rock City.
dues: Dues varies.  From high $600 range to the Low $700.
cities: You name a city in the world, we likely have members. We have been around for over 16 years.

name: Glowskull Asylum Int'l
description: We bring extreme metal to the playa, the one and only 24/7 metal bar in BRC!
duties: Run the bar as much as they can, come early if possible or stay for strike.
cities: Santa Cruz, Paso Robles, Austin, Portland, Prague CZ

name: Here
description: What do we do? We’re a breakfast sandwich drive through. Bracing that Atlantic divide, we’re half European half American. A small but hardy bunch.
duties:
"Three shifts during the week, this could be one of the events, helping cook brunch or dinner, or doing an ice run for example. 

It's also mandatory to either help with build or tear down. "
dues: $750 - This covers all food, water, power, shade infrastructure, (need to buy new parts every year), and transport of the camp to BRC. (U-haul and pickup rental). As well as event supplies!
cities: New York, San Francisco, Amsterdam, Berlin

name: Hibernaculum
description: We have been an international camp until this year when must attend weddings and other obstacles appeared. We are a loving, friendly, serendipitous group with lots of toys to play on and no dramas. Our big beautiful canopy is to share.
size: 13 so far with a couple of maybe's in the wings.
duties: Pitch in as needed, assist with activities. Feminine photographer wanted.
dues: $100 goes to equipment maintenance and current expenses
cities: Denver, Grand Junction, Salida CO, Mariposa CA, Seattle WA, Austin TX, Cornville, AZ

name: Horny Camp
description: We bring polymer clay to the playa and teach burners how to make little polymer clay horns to wear on their heads. We're a friendly camp that prides itself on making a safe, relaxed home for all of our members.
duties: To work 3 3-hour shifts doing horn-making or one of our other activities.
dues: $350. Dues go towards: our shipping container storage and delivery fees, the greywater infrastructure that needs to be purchased yearly, wear and tear on camp infrastructure, our polymer clay and other supplies needed to do our activities, and trash disposal costs. All dues moneies go directly to the camp. No one is paid to run the camp.
cities: New York City, Austin, Bay Area, Boston, Reno

name: House of Skin
description: We are a sunscreen and chapstick camp. I also bring a trampoline, stage with striper pole, speaker, a shaded camp area at the front of camp, a fire pit and a lot of fun
duties: To help run the camps sunscreen statiin for at least one shift (maybe more depending on how many campers are in camp and help with at least a st one camp event. Also, clean up after yourself all around camp and in the kitchen. Not much else
dues: Nobe/none
cities: Spokane, WA and Reno. NV

name: I love elephants
description: I Love Elephants is a visionary Burning Man camp on the Esplanade offering immersive art, music, movement, and connection. In BRC 2026, we will host the Alex Grey Gallery in a new pyramid, alongside a Visionary Arts Dome, ecstatic dance, downtempo DJs, yoga, and workshops. Our Love Bazaar will provide a 3,000-square-foot shaded, padded space for gathering, practice, and play.
duties: Total of 8 hour volunteer shift
dues: $750 - toward 3 meals per day and interactivity
cities: Oregon, Colorado and the world

name: Indigo's Speakeasy
description: I am a veteran burner who loves sharing single malt. I have a modest camp of 1 with room to host 2 virgins that come radically self reliant.
duties: Share a good dram and conversation.
dues: none
cities: SF Bay Area

name: ING
description: Camp ING is a long-running, international Burning Man camp built around community, contribution, hospitality, art, music, and shared effort. We create a welcoming home base in Black Rock City: a place to land, connect, reset, participate, and have a real group on playa without losing your independence. Our camp hosts daily interactivity, communal shade, social rituals, and camp-led events with a playful, intentional, self-reliant vibe. No sherpas, no spectators, just people building and sharing something worth returning to.
duties: We use a simple three-point participation system. Every camper is expected to contribute at least three points through camp duties such as build, strike, MOOP sweeps, kitchen help, event support, water/greywater help, and general camp care. Build and strike carry extra weight because they are the most labor-intensive and are critical to making the camp happen. The goal is not to over-schedule people, but to make sure everyone participates, no one gets treated like a guest, and the camp stays functional, welcoming, and Leave No Trace.
dues: Camp dues are $250 before June 26 and $300 after June 26. Dues go directly toward the real hard costs of making camp happen: our shipping container, storage, transport to and from playa, shared shade, kitchen infrastructure, water and greywater systems, power/fuel, tools, safety supplies, decor, interactivity, and general camp maintenance. There are no paid staff, no profit, and nobody is paid for organizing the camp. We simply add up the shared expenses and divide them across the campers. Dues do not include a Burning Man ticket, vehicle pass, personal food, personal shelter, or personal supplies.
cities: South Lake Tahoe, Eugene Oregon, San Diego, Las Vegas.

name: Kentucky Fried Camp
description: 📌About us: We’ve been around for about 12 years and next year was always better. 📍Location: We’re at 6&C this year! 🥃 Gift: We serve breakfast every morning which is a Fried Baloney Sandwich with a side shot of Kentucky Bourbon. Sounds gross, tastes delicious.
duties: Participation in our breakfast service 9am-11am Tues-Fri 10am-12pm Sat, general participation with set up and strike
cities: Lexington, Boston, New York City, Milan (Italy), Yakima, Cincinatti

name: Lands Beyond
description: Lands Beyond is a cosmic haven for strivers and adventurers always seeking the unknown. Lands Beyond is an international camp with Mexican roots and flair. Our community thrives on diversity, exploration and curiosity. We are not tourists in Playa.
duties:
"Lands Beyond runs on participation, not spectatorship. Four light commitments keep the whole station aloft — and they're half the fun.
1. Raise the station: lend your hands to setup or strike. We conjure a whole world from bare dust and return it to dust — be part of the magic at one end or the other.
2. Feed the crew: cook one communal meal across the week. Fuel for fellow space travelers, served with love under the desert sky.
3. Gift the playa: just 1 shift of interactivity for the entire burn. Share your spark — a skill, a ritual, a moment of wonder for passing dust-dwellers.
4. Hold the orbit: pitch in on a few small team tasks through the week. Many hands keep the mothership humming and the family thriving."
dues: Lands Beyond is a non profit organization. The camp fee is $3,800, and the funding goes towards paying for the myriad of things that we bring for campers and for the Burning Man community alike. We make huge expenses to bring the most amazing interactivity and community we can and all the funds go towards paying for those costs and nothing else.
cities: Mexico City, San Fancisco, others

name: Legends of the Hidden Playa
description: We are approximately 25 people in our 30s and 40s who run an obstacle course/escape room type adventure that's structured like the old Nickelodeon show, but themed around Burning Man satire. We enjoy having a chill home based that allows us to enjoy the city to its fullest while having somewhere comfortable to land when we need a break.
duties: We ask everyone in camp to do 2 shifts running our maze. The shifts are 3 hours long.
dues: Our cost share is $300/person. This covers water delivery, shipping and storage for our container of communal infrastructure, and all the other odds and ends of camp expenses.
cities: Austin, TX and Los Angeles, CA

name: M*A*S*H 4207th
description: Low-key and mellow camp. Bar during the day and the TV Show MASH at night.
duties: Help with set-up and tear-down is appreciated. A bar shift and/or an ice run helps a lot.
dues: $50, 1 bottle of vodka, 2 cases of PBR
cities: Portland OR, Phoenix AZ

name: Math Camp @ Group W
description: A _SAFE_ place to Drink and DErive. We are nerdy/techy/DIY/maker kind of group based mostly in the Bay Area.
duties: Help setup/strike. Help prep/serve/cleanup Pie Time. Run the Bar. Host a lecture.
dues: $50 encouraged for pie service. Drinks you want at the bar.
cities: East Palo Alto, San Francisco, Truckee

name: Maybe You Should Talk To Someone
description: We’re a heart-centered, conscious community offering on-playa counseling to help fellow Burners unf*ck their burn. You do not need to be a therapist to apply! We do ask that all campmates are either currently in therapy or have been in therapy before. We’re building a camp where people value self-awareness, accountability, and emotional growth.
duties: Each campmate commits to two volunteer shifts Monday-Friday. Shifts entails either counseling or helping with camp maintenance.We also require that all campmates participate in strike, Saturday morning.
dues: $450. They go toward water delivery, camp infrastructure, storage and transportation, power, and ice.
cities: Los Angeles, San Francisco, Joshua Tree, Nashville, Austin, Grants Pass

name: MESA Camp
description: Join us atop the MESA, your place to escape the chaos and find connection. Join us for nightly mezcal tastings, drum circles, volleyball and late night deep playa rides on our mutant vehicle, Garden of Dreamin'
duties: Each camper is required to do a MOOP shift, a kitchen shift and an interactivity shift of your choice
dues: Dues are $2,500 per person which covers:   Shower, kitchen, community support, art car, ebike discounts, MESA lounge w/ sound system, mezcal bar, camp trash removal, build team sponsorships, annual storage/transpo, insurance, and accounting/tax costs
cities: LA, SF, NYC, London, Zurich, Amsterdam, Paris, and more!

name: MindShark
description: MindShark has large scale art including an interactive LED dome, multiple flame effects, wicked sound system and DJ setup, and the worst/best bar on playa (depending on who you ask). Dues go towards our interactivity as well as two meals a day, drinking water, shade for tents, shaded hanging-out space, and all the booze from our bar. If you're a builder, artist, maker, flow artist, DJ, musician, cook, bartenders, organizer, or generally passionate about something, we want you! (We're also looking for people interested in early arrival to help with build.) Placed at Esplanade & 3:00 for 2026, apply to join at CampMindShark.com!
duties: Total of 12-15 hours of shifts throughout the week. 3 event shifts, 3 chore shifts, 1 kitchen
dues: $550.69, Dues go towards the camp interactivity;  the led art, flame effects and running the bar, as well as meals, water, shade and other camp infrastructure and logistics. Dues for the year are determined by our budget dividend by the expected number of campers.
cities: Denver, San Francisco, NYC, Seattle, Portland

name: Mona Camp
description: We are building four art pieces on playa: Mona Moon, Correfoc, WiFi Jail and Ragnarok
duties: We are looking for builders with art-building experience that are able to come a few days before Burn week to help us support our four art pieces
dues: depends
cities: SF, Berkeley, China

name: Moon Landing
description: We are an art car and art support camp.. We have two art cars and a placed art project. We focus on building community through shared effort in physically participating in building the city.
duties: We have a few spots available for build week participants, general camp up keep, art car daily cleaning, art project servicing, final strike and teardown.
dues: $500, water, showers, shade, private portos, camp infastrcture, art car repairs, camp equipment
cities: Oakland, San Francisco, Poland, Costa Rica

name: Mostly in Tune
description: Leave with dusty shoes and a song stuck in your head. Mostly in Tune is a participatory jam camp. Join the band, borrow an instrument, sing along, enjoy live band karaoke, perform spoken word over live backing tracks, or simply enjoy the music. No experience required.
duties: Help build and strike camp, pitch in with shared camp chores, and help create a welcoming, inclusive environment. Musicians are always welcome to join the music. No pressure, no expectations.
cities: Santa Cruz, CA

name: Naked Heart
description: We are a welcoming community who come together to present 140 workshops on playa each year. We are cultural monkey-wrenchers who are prototyping new ways for humans to relate to each other with love and kindness. We welcome new-comers and international campers. Our program explores topics of sensuality, shame-busting, human potential, movement, sexuality, Goddess awareness, somatic, dance, and more!!
duties: Each member must contribute 3 work shifts (about 2 hours each)
dues: Dues increase as it gets close to the Burn. Currently they are $1250. This gets you a full meal plan, shade for every tent, communal cooled spaces, water for all needs including showers, a steam bath, power, etc. The camp dues also pay for our interactivity costs. Join soon, Dues go up 7/15 & 8/1
cities: We are truly international but have a lot of campers from California Bay Area and Boulder, CO

name: Netsah
description: NETSAH Rising Sanctuary is a grounded, relaxing, and welcoming camp built around connection, beauty, and good vibes. ✨ Inspired by oriental aesthetics, we created a peaceful oasis in the desert where Burners can slow down, recharge, and enjoy meaningful moments together. Our space blends music, art, comfort, and community, offering a calm energy that balances the intensity of the playa. 🏜️🎶 We believe in sharing, authentic human connections, and creating a temporary home where everyone feels welcome.
duties:
"All camp members are expected to practice Leave No Trace principles and actively help keep our space clean, organized, and MOOP-free throughout the event.

We also encourage everyone to contribute to our community by helping with our morning coffee and breakfast hosting, welcoming guests, and supporting the positive and relaxing atmosphere that defines NETSAH Rising Sanctuary. Small contributions from everyone help create a beautiful experience for all."
dues: Camp dues range from 990€ to 1,300€ per person, depending on the level of contribution.  These dues go towards:  Water refills and grey water pumping 🚰 Internet for the camp 📡 Camp logistics, infrastructure, and setup 🏕️ Morning breakfast (coffee, pancakes, and hosting) ☕🥞  This helps ensure NETSAH Rising Sanctuary runs smoothly and provides a comfortable, well-supported experience for all camp members.
cities: PARIS / MIAMI / NYC / TEL AVIV

name: Never Sleep Again
description: We are a camp that fosters connection by producing quality parties featuring some of the best musical artists on the playa from around the world in an intimate and decadent setting.
duties: We seek folks that are contributors not spectators.
cities: Los Ángeles. San Francisco, Berlin

name: Node
description: Node is a desert oasis.a place to escape the heat, relax, dance, drink coffee or enjoy our 4th annual hip hop bbq!
duties: Everyone is expecting to contribute. Everyone will have another 5 (1-2 hour shifts).
cities: New York! LA! New Zealand!

name: Not A Cult
description: We are a wellness camp that jokes at religion. We are alcohol free and enjoy yoga, mocktails, and bits of debotchery
duties: Each member must help with 3-4 events or dinners. Each shift is from 60-90 minutes and is low stress and hopefully fun. We expect everyone to help with take down on Sunday. We expect everyone to show up ready to Burn.
dues: $300-400 goes toward our storage, shower upgrades, new equipment, paying off old debts (last year we build a lot of shade), shared camp meals, many other things.
cities: Northern Arizona, Bay Area California

name: Ocean Beach
description: Ocean Beach is a sun-soaked fever dream of surf shacks, sea creatures, and boardwalk absurdity. Join our salty circus for lobster pizza rituals, smoothie shamans, sunset cocktail ceremonies, and the infamous ode to JB's cheeseburger in paradise. Stretch your soul with yoga, tantra, and sound healing. Play boardwalk games, ride silly rides, perform on our aerial hoops, or lounge like a sea god.
duties: participate in camp chores and interactive programming
dues: $600. Pays for all food, water, drinks. Helps pay for interactive programming.
cities: New York, San Francisco, Brussels, Los Angeles

name: Olympvs
description: Together we're creating an immersive experience that invites the playa into a space of myth, ritual, curiosity, and sensual exploration. Through performance, interaction, hospitality, and play, we want to create moments people will remember long after the dust settles.The infrastructure to support both our community and our gift: — A stretch-tent lounge with deep shade and space to gather — A secret speakeasy bar and dust-proof lounge for respite from the storms — Nightly Mediterranean feasts that bring our camp together — A power grid for your RV — Camp drinking water and scheduled RV refills and pump-outs — Eco-friendly bathrooms We're looking for people who want to build, perform, host, create, and participate. People who value community as much as comfort, and understand that the best experiences on playa are the ones we make together.
duties: Members commit to 6 hours of shifts during the week + strike. Participation is how Olympvs lives and thrives.
dues: $2500 or below depending on group size and other details. Olympvs runs on shared contribution, not services. Camp dues represent each member’s share of infrastructure: containers, shade, water, power, bathrooms, food, and ambiance.
cities: LA-based, with campmates from NYC, Europe, and beyond.

name: Orphan Endorphin
description: We build BETTER BURNERS!
duties: Setup and Strike
dues: $125 for Ticket Aid recipients, $225 for Endorphins, $300 for Orphans
cities: Denver, San Francisco, Sacramento, L.A., London, Buenos Aires, Orlando

name: People's Art Congress
description: People's Art Congress is a participatory art camp seeking members who can contribute without drama and with strong leave-no-trace habits.
duties: No drama, no moop
dues: None

name: Pepperland
description: Beatles based camp. Very mellow. Have an event you want to do? Pepperland is the place!
duties: Some daily chores in camp most less than ten minutes. Just keeping up the cleanliness of the camp and ice runs
dues: $300. Goes towards camp stuff only and we supply an evening meal daily
cities: Sacramento, England, all over the states and world!

name: Playa Agora
description: Playa Agora is a warm and friendly camp that loves bringing people closer together. We host interactive workshops focused on deep human connection and community. If you love meeting wonderful people and building real relationships, our camp will feel like home.
duties: We believe in working together as a team to make the camp run smoothly. Every camp mate helps out for about 8 hours total throughout the entire week. You can choose to help guide our connection workshops, or you can help build the camp structures or take them down at the end. It is a fun, shared effort that brings us all closer together.
dues: Our camp dues are $550. Every dollar goes directly into shared comforts like a large shade structure, a camp shower, and a kitchen. It also covers daily needs like water, generator fuel, kitchen propane, gray water removal, and waste disposal. By pooling our money, we build a safe and cozy home in the dust for everyone.
cities: That would be a long list. We are an international group, but you can easily meet many of us in person. In the US: Denver, Los Angeles, and Minneapolis are a few of the cities.

name: Playa Choir
description: Playa Choir is an all inclusive Art Performance Camp. Playa Choir is a rowdy, non-dogmatic inspirational experience! We have an adult choir and this year we are starting a children's choir as well. We perform at the Burning Man Opening Ceremony, with the Black Rock Philharmonic, in the Burning Man Procession to the Man on Burn Night, Sunrise at the Temple and in the Playa Choir Dome on Burn Sunday.
duties: Everyone at Playa Choir Camp supports our art project (The Choir) in some way by either singing in the choir, playing in the band, or being a Greeter.  Greeters are an important part of our interactivity as all members of the choir and band are busy during rehearsals.  Greeters serve coffee in the morning and invite passers by to come on in to relax and watch or even to join in.  In addition to supporting the art project, all campers participate in camp duties such as mooping,  sweeping the common area or  ice runs.  Playa Choir is not a camp to join if you want to just put in a few shifts.  But if you are passionate about music and want to BE the art- this is the place for you.
dues: Dues  are from $200 and up depending on what  sort of site- Inside the shade structure vs RV out on the grounds.  ALL Dues go towards supporting the art project.
cities: We are from all over the world.  We zoom every week and invite prospective members to come and meet the crew.  We meet on Sundays at 4:30 Pacific Time.      Join Our Zoom Meeting! Meeting ID: 556 439 7636 Passcode: rxELJ5

name: Red Nose District
description: 20 year Circus Theme camp! Offering circus school, hijinks, and a welcoming atmosphere
duties: Everybody works, everybody pays!
cities: Bend, Portland, LA, Santa Fe, NYC

name: RedNeck Regal Theatre
description: We offer a sharing of visual theater through interactive hand puppet show and movie nights. It is all themed and usually offered with a story or experience
duties: We would expect to find lovers of movies or movie genres to be able to share favorite movies with a personal story that makes the experience unique to our attendees on their assigned movie nights. The theater puppets is just as easy as reading a bedtime story.
dues: No dues
cities: Reno, NV and Odessa, TX

name: Reverbia
description: We are The Portal of Live Music at Burning Man. If you like live music and a balanced harmonious community who acculturalizes and mentors New Campers and Birgens with patience and kindness, asks for no hard drugs and responsible partying (several people in our camp do the Burn mostly sober), has quiet hours in our camping areas, requests respect and NO DRAMA…then we may be a good fit. We also have many solo campers and are a consent camp who practices the 10 principles. We offer vegan, vegetarian, and restricted diet meals in our omnivorous kitchen, along with 24/7 snack/nuts/frutis/sandwich making/popcorn/coffee/tea area.
duties: Our gift will be about 125 hours of live music programming. We ask that all Reverbians volunteer 12-14 hours of service towards our community needs, which is programming/workshop/concert support, kitchen, LNT, DPW, Placement, Build Team (set it up), Exit Team (watch the Playa return to its natural state).  We also ask that Reversions assist on our All tear down day, which goes super fast as many hands make light work. Everyone moops a packs out their own trash.
dues: $480 is our basic cost sharing price to camp with us.  This includes basic camping, first come first serve tent shade under 6 major structures, basic power, drinking water, showers, sink, 3 meals a day + snack/nut/fruits/sandwich 24/7 area.  RVs, Camping vehicles, larger tents are additional.  ($60-$450 for week pending vehicle, size. and power needs)
cities: Eugene, Vancouver, BC, Portland, SF.

name: Rooftop Frose
description: We are a small camp that sets up a ten foot high platform with an eight foot shad above to have a max of six burners at a time enjoy the view from the platform. Rooftop Frose is open M,T,Th &F from a little before sunset until a little after the sun goes down and the stars come out serving Froses to our guests. I am from Tucson Arizona and would be interested in one or two burners joining us from Tucson. No dues or central kitchen must bring everything you need with you to the playa.
duties: Help serving and making Froses for our guests and build and tear down of scaffold and camp
dues: NA
cities: Tucson, San Francisco and Denver

name: Rootpile
description: We love to burn down some fast fiddle tunes in the evening with some moonshine but we also hang out as a community to support each other every single day. We have a wonderful camp that is full of creatives, musicians, artists, and builders.
duties: We only expect everyone to volunteer one full day during the week and they are free to explore the playa for the remainder of the event.
dues: Dues are $500 per person and go toward our shared resources like trash removal, diesel generator, grid upgrades, meal ingredients, water, showers, bathrooms, shade structures, sound equipment rental, dance floor, Texas BBQ Stage, stage props, coffee, electrolytes, plates/forks
cities: Austin, San Fran, Reno, Denver, Houston, Asheville

name: Secret Passage
description: We're a scrappy group of mostly New Yorkers burning since 2011, offering a variety of theme camp shenanigans in our hosting tent. Organized and resourceful, we provide all the essential playa needs while keeping dues reasonable. We work and play hard, enjoy volunteering as greeters and adventuring as a group. We're grounded, supportive, collaborative, and seeking likeminded folks to join our ranks this year.
duties: Attend remaining planning meetings, sign up for a few camp staffing shifts, help lead a project area (transportation, interior design, meals, etc), commit to either build or strike (but preferably both). Work collaboratively to get it done.
dues: ~$700 cost share covers all communalized costs indicted above (including transport to/from playa!)
cities: NYC mostly

name: Serenity Now
description: We are a small, central coffee house near the middle of black rock city. We have a beautiful dome as our shared space, as well as shade for campers, a shower setup, unlimited water and some really incredible people. We are a very relaxed camp, and our primary focus is being a welcome-to-all joyous space for people to start their day, or recover after a long night! Serenity Now is designed to offer the perfect blend of community and self-expression.
size: Up to 30, but we are usually a smaller crew
duties: Members get to host a coffee service in the morning, but it is mostly just being present and awake to make friends (the coffee is effectively self-serve). We have a few other events and always encourage member involvement, but they are all optional.
dues: $350 per person. Dues cover public gift coffee and water, and camp water, power, trash, showers, etc. Dues are used exclusively to refresh camp and purchase the years' coffee/water/etc.
cities: Lake Tahoe, Los Angeles, potentially SF, Boulder, CO and more. We have a few nomads in camp.

name: Short Bus Coffee Camp
description: Short Bus offers premium Kona coffee harvested and roasted especially for the burn! We serve 20 gallons daily, starting from 0800 until daily supplies run out. We also provide organic cream and raw sugar and a daily assortment of breakfast pastries. This year you’ll find us at 8:00 & A 😊
duties: Short Bus campers usually work one or two mornings during the burn, serving coffee for about 2-3 hours.  The rest of the burn is yours!!
dues: Dues are minimal, $100 per camper.  This covers the cost of coffee, filtered water, cream/creamers/oat milk, pastries/breakfast bars, and booze for Irish coffees
cities: We’re based out of Chico, CA

name: Slutty Microphone
description: We're 24/7 live music jam camp with a cozy always-on open mic stage, with a lot of instruments, welcoming bands and solo performers. We usually accommodate 50-100 guest performers per week. We also host nightly dinners, spontaneous midnight breakcore raves, and sunrise barrel side therapy sessions. We're artists, dancers, musicians, doctors, software and hardware engineers in ~25-4x age range.
duties: Any combination of: cooking, cleaning, infrastructure maintenance tasks, MC and/or play music at the stage. Serious effort projects (like towing a cargo trailer with your truck) can get you up to 100% camp fee discount.
dues: $450-800, depending on time of joining and contributions
cities: San Francisco, Berkeley, New York, Montreal

name: Snack Shack
description: Snack Shack is a place where people come to get some sustenance in the shade. This year we are located at 2:45 & B serving hot dogs, grilled cheese and cold beverages Monday-Thursday from 5 to 7pm. On Wednesday we host the World Famous Black Rock City Hotdog Eating Contest as well as a sensual hotdog eating contest.
duties: Help setup and/or breakdown camp. Participate in 3 out of 4 of our food services.
dues: $250. Dues help pay for storage of camp infrastructure and food/beverages for service.
cities: Bellingham, Seattle, Portland, NYC, Asheville

name: Souilshakers
description: Souilshakers is a camp built around a photo bus art car and lively camp day parties. Members help with setup, build, strike, and party hosting while dues support the art car and events.
duties: To help with setup & build/strike. Camp day parties
dues: $350 towards our photo bus Artcar/Camp parties
cities: Bend Or, Vancouver BC, Queenstown NZ

name: Spanky's Wine Bar
description: Spanky's is legend on the playa. 2026 will be our 19th year. We take a light-hearted approach to kink, with a focus on humor in its lowest forms. With attractions such as the Spank-O-Matic, the Twisted Nipple, and Trigger the Wonder Pony, Spanky's has staked a claim to be the beating heart of the Red Light District.
duties: Give at least one fuck about the success of the camp.   And 16 hours of shift work.
dues: 500.00.  Fuel, booze, toys, infrastructure, storage
cities: Reno, Bay Area, Europe,

name: Stag Camp
description: Stag Camp is a tight-knit, come-as-you-are community defined by radical acceptance, self-reliance, and an easygoing spirit. We ditch mandatory dues and rigid shifts—relying instead on voluntary donations, mutual respect, and a collective love for the Burn. It’s a home for the free-spirited to leave default-world stressors behind, roam freely, and find family.
duties: Radical reliance.  Must clean your MOOP.
dues: Storage fees and keeping the Stag alive!
cities: Utah, Ohio, New York, California, Massachusetts

name: Stardust Lounge
description: Since 2008, Stardust Lounge has offered visitors a fun blend of rituals, games, and opportunities for self-care. We are a group of ever growing friends that has grown over they years. You do not just camp with Stardust, you become part of the tribe. We offer a wedding chapel where we marry anybody to anyone or to anything.
size: 15 with Stardust.  We are a Hub with two other camps: Mellow Mountain and Horny Camp
duties: We expect our "New Stars" to bring their best adventurous and friendly selves, to participate in our camp social adventures and help where they can.  We all pitch in clean, entertain, play and organize.  We want new Stardusters to experience Burningman at its fullest and to sink themselves into the experience.  The only duty and responsibility you will have is to take over and run the camp on Friday with all the other "New Stars".
dues: Typically run about $300-$350 and covers food, snacks, water, drinks and infrastructure.
cities: San Francisco, Oakland, San Juan PR, Paris France, Berlin Germany and more

name: StarFist
description: We are an eclectic, international tribe of Burners of all walks of life, all ages and all sensibilities who converge in the dust to revive our faith in humanity. Our heartfelt primary offering is our STARFIST INFAMOUS INTERNATIONAL TEA SERVICE where we delight the citizens of BRC with refreshments and loving hospitality. We also have a SECRET SPA and offer various other activities in our glorious tea tent, such as daytime WELLNESS SESSIONS and some night time events. This year, we have our most public facing camp placement ever and are looking forward to more traffic so we are excited to add some new gifts for the playa including a NIGHT TEA SERVICE.
duties: 1 camp work day and 2 x 4 hour strike shifts the day before you leave
dues: This year’s dues are $650 (or more if people want to contribute to subsidise low income dues, which we offer).  Your dues cover the cost of our whole camp infrastructure from storage and transportation to our camp offering and everything you might need to enjoy your home in the desert for that dusty week.  We have a full (container) kitchen, separate coffee station, water for everyone, communal ice runs and spare coolers if needed. A communal dining and lounge area as well as our luxurious tea tent which is always available to everyone. The kitchen is stocked with basic condiments and we have fresh produce delivery including eggs. We have two official camp meals and the rest of the time everyone just communally cooks together for whoever is around and hungry. We have a bar with a decent selection of spirits, mixers and obscure liquors from around the world. We also have a solar powered charging station for phones and gadgets and relatively functional showers.
cities: Bay Area, London, New Orleans, LA, NY and scattered all over the world

name: Team Human Base Camp
description: We are a small eclectic camp that believes in community and artistic expression. We have a small stage and welcome performances of all kinds, as well as hosting Shit Show Karaoke. We have members from California to Indiana, Texas to Australia. But above all we are a community.
duties: Everyone is responsible for cooking at least one meal for the camp (pair up for this task). Everyone is required to contribute time to either camp build, camp breakdown or stage operations during burn week.

name: Temple Oasis
description: We are a support camp for Temple Guardian volunteers, keeping a calm space to promote connection and rest. Ours is a global and all-ages community, encompassing people from a multitude of professions and passions. We provide shade, water, a shower, a kitchen, and graywater collection. We host one mid-week evening event for the Temple Guardian community.
duties: Be a registered Temple Guardian and serve three shifts at the Temple. Participate in light camp operational duties, primarily Leave No Trace efforts. All campers partake in camp strike Saturday. Not a party camp - maintain a calm environment promoting rest for TGs
dues: $250 - covers water, porta-potties, graywater collection, and shade/infrastructure
cities: Global - not a city-based camp

name: The Burning Globe
description: We are a (very-loosely) Shakespeare themed theatre camp. We have a theatre space where we host performances, workshops and parties. But wait there's more! Behind our theatre is our Aged Elf Speakeasy, a medieval fantasy inspired bit bar (think "Prancing Pony" or the tavern where a DnD campaign may begin).
duties: Varies, but mostly general camp responsibilities: Moop sweeps, bar shifts, ice runs, build/teardown, etc.
dues: $350 - Covers our trailer and container, communal water and infrastructure
cities: New York, Los Angeles, Seattle, Boise

name: The cage
description: A curated community of DJs, entrepreneurs, fitness experts, creatives, and experienced burners—sharing one unforgettable week in Black Rock City. Designed for those who value connection, adventure, and elevated comfort without losing the essence of the Playa. If this feels like your tribe, we’d love to meet you.
duties: MOOP, supply management, participation in all camp activities
dues: Helping...
cities: Guadalajara, México. New York City

name: The Club
description: The Club is a playful space in Black Rock City centered around a Joker-run casino, where blackjack and roulette become interactive games that bring strangers together, make them laugh, and pull them into the show. Together, we’re creating a shared playa space with shade, a lounge, bar, fire pit, kitchen, and casino where we can gather, host, play, rest, and bring our weird little gift to the city.
duties: 2 shifts across, game tent, kitchen, build week, DJ, maintenance.
dues: $1000. Dues help cover shared camp costs like meals, shade, kitchen, power/fuel, lighting, decor, games, lounge setup, safety supplies, transportation, and logistics.
cities: New York City

name: The Fluffy Cloud
description: Check out our application link! Has all the info!
duties: Cost share and 6-8 hours of shifts during week
dues: $2500 or below depending on group size and other details. Dues covered shared costs of camp and gift.
cities: Worldwide

name: The HeeBeeGeeBee Healers
description: The HeeBeeGeeBee Healers have been Black Rock City's grounded, healing oasis since 2000. We offer healing services such as massage, energy work, coaching, and talk therapy on Tuesday through Friday, and have ongoing workshops in our Classroom tent all day on topics related to healing, connection, and personal development.
duties: All campers volunteer for three hours per day (with one full day off) in one of four Circles: Healing Circle - offer 3 hours of healing services per day; Fairy/Greeter Circle - welcome our visitors and help orient them to our services and workshops for 3 hours per day; Kitchen Circle - prepare, serve and clean up for one meal (3 hours) per day; and Infrastructure - help maintain our camp's structural integrity and deal with trash and recycling for 3 hours per day.
dues: $675 which goes toward three meals per day and snacks, all your water needs, and one shower per day (except in cases of weather or resource emergency). We also provide a shade structure that goes over all individual camper tents, and multiple shaded areas to hang out in.
cities: San Francisco, Boulder, New York City, Vancouver, Reno, Washington D.C.

name: The Lost Penguins
description: The Lost Penguins is a hospitality camp and we are celebrating our 25th anniversary this year. We are open from 11am to 2am. We serve snow cones, licorice, dark chocolate, penguin pee (vodka & lemonade), virgin lemonade, cold water, live and DJ music, a large, shaded space, and a cuddle puddle. We are an international camp, and this year we represent nine countries.
duties: Four easy volunteer work shifts. For example, kitchen, snow cones, bar, cafe host, LNT, ice run. Roughly 2-3 hour shifts.
dues: $385 per person -- daily breakfast & dinner, camp water, camp shower w/hot water, Starlink access, RV & tent space; power & pump-outs extra.
cities: San Francisco bay area, several states within the U.S., Vancouver, Toronto, Mexico City... more

name: The makers space
description: The makers space is an art support camp offering classes for members and artists while helping install art across Black Rock City. Camp dues cover water, waste, and nightly dinner.
duties: Each member with have camp shifts and also help install art
dues: 500 pays for water and waste. Dinner each night
cities: Nevada  idaho  Oregon

name: The Sound Garden
description: High-vibe sound camp with a 360° system—DJs and performers by night; sound healings, deep house yoga, listening parties, bodywork, and healing workshops by day in the Garden Lounges.

name: The Sultry Lodge
description: A sultry crowd from 5 countries. We speak English, German, Russian, Spanish. If you want to be friends with your camp mates, not just share facilities, get in touch.
duties: Help set-up and tear-down, 3h help with activities, keep the camp tidy. Optional: Build, Load/unload truck (Seattle).
dues: $550. All common structures (living quarters, public pavilion, kitchen, shower); Truck rent + gas; Storage of everything through the year; Supplies for interactivities; Trash disposal; Gas for Power.
cities: Seattle area

name: Twilight Language
description: A collective of creators on a mission to realize visual art and cultivate energy around it. Bold, interactive, innovative, novel—or maybe it’s just the world’s best pizza party.
duties: Shifts—for everyone—run on a point system(Less desirable shifts garner more points)
dues: Shade camping (with strike): $800 — includes all camp services and participation in post- event strike, Shade camping (without strike): $1200 — for campmates opting out of the tear-down, RV space: $5,000 per RV, covering up to three people per RV (strike penalty does not apply to RV campers we encourage you to participate in the strike, but do understand RV rental timelines)
cities: Bay Area, Los Angeles

name: U.F.O.
description: U.F.O. is an oasis by day to pamper yourself and relax in a cozy, shaded environment with great music. At night we turn up the bass and the fire. Lots of personality.
size: We are aiming to have 30-45 campers and have room for new faces this year!
duties:
"Our signature events are: fire archery on Tuesday and Thursday night (exactly what it sounds like), and our big spa day on Wednesday (several hours of music, frozé, treats, ice cold face masks, etc to treat weary participants with joy).

Would ask for help on Spa day and 1-2 other shifts throughout the week including a fairly easy tidying up the camp shift. 

In addition dues cover a strike deposit and we ask that you help with strike (this is our most effective way to ensure everyone helps out).

Also we have power for everything but AC and water for cooking and drinking all event but not enough for dedicated showers. "
dues: $450 + $150 strike deposit that gets refunded. Dues to towards all of the awesome shade structure, futons, sound system for our rotating DJs to play on, communal kitchen (gas stove, pizza ovens, multiple fridges and deep freezers powered by generators). It also goes towards acquiring a large amount of new shade structure for the camper portion to keep everyone cool. Happy to chat more details!
cities: Bay Area, Seattle, Portland, Denver, Houston

name: VICE
description: We are the oldest queer camp (primarily gay-identifying) on the playa, having provided ice cold Vietnamese Iced Coffee Experiences (VICE) for almost 24 yrs! We’re an all-inclusive, low-frills camp with minimal duties (two 4-hr volunteer shits), low dues, and max welcoming atmosphere, right in the middle of the queerborhood.
duties: Everyone is expected to pick up two volunteer shifts (4 hrs each), of which one is coffee or tea service, and to support in camp breakdown. Other than that we expect people to be welcoming and supportive :)
dues: $200 – goes to coffee supplies, frontage, one communal camp dinner, transporting camp materials
cities: SF, New York, LA

name: Waffles & House
description: A syrupy-sweet crew of burners who serve up hot waffles, cold cocktails , bumping house music, and memories to last a lifetime.
duties: Everyone does three shifts- 1 waffle or bartending shift , 1 dinner shift, and 1 WaffOlympics shift! It’s technically not a “shift”, but everyone in camp has to participate in a day of striking camp at the end of the week and making sure our plot is fully picked clean of all MOOP!
dues: Our dues are sliding scale, but the base dues are $995. If you can pay more, the extra money goes to our scholarship fund, which helps campers who cannot pay the 995 in full. All camp dues goes to cover the cost of our amenities, their storage, and transport.
cities: We are mostly based in the SF Bay Area, LA, and NYC! But we have waffles living all over the USA and internationally! We gather together for fun events not infrequently! Come join the family!

name: Wild Child
description: Wild Circus humans bringing an epic apparatus 6:00 A we love to perform and party 🔥
duties: We keep our duties minimal but you are expected to help and bring good vibes when you can
dues: Free this year - if you help us build and tear down
cities: Bali, Phoenix, Vegas

name: Witche's Brew
description: we are witches brew, we offer tea cnad coffee service to black rock city, as well as hosting pyro coven and a nightly performance area
dues: 100$ per person to operate camp infrastructure and the coffee service
cities: Tulsa Oklahoma & Las Vegas nevada

name: Word Play Cafe
description: Word Play Cafe is the space at Burning Man dedicated entirely to word games for people to gather, play and learn together. We have a 30 foot dome where we host our custom and classic favorite word games and word game events. We have a small, inclusive camp. Some of our camp mates have been coming to burning man for a very long time and some are newer.
duties:
"Campmates help with build and/or strike and daily mooping of the camp and Word Play Cafe. Campers do shifts in the dome where they hand out games, play with visitors or help run events. In 2026, our hours will be Monday-Friday, 10am - 4pm and we expect camp mates to work at least 2 three hour shifts. Responsibilities for dome shifts can vary among camp mates based on other contributions to the camp and hub, and other unique factors. Campmates are expected to be respectful of others and help keep communal spaces clean and tidy. 

We occasionally bring Word Play Cafe out into the city - these are optional activities and may or may not be scheduled. There is a lot of room for creativity and contribution. In addition to the work we do on playa, we create games and puzzles during the year to bring to the event, and you are welcome to participate in that if it’s your idea of fun. 
"
dues: ~$200: Water, greywater, portos, solar power
cities: SF Bay Area & Atlanta

name: XUZA camp
description: XUZA is a long-running mutant vehicle camp built around bringing music, immersive visuals, flame effects, and connection to Black Rock City. Our campmates help create and operate an interactive art experience that takes XUZA out into the city and deep playa to share with everyone who finds us. We welcome first-time Burners and love watching that transition unfold throughout the week. Campmates who DJ in the default world will be scheduled deck time on XUZA during the week, while everyone has opportunities to be involved in the build, operations, art, support crew, and the moments we create for BRC.
duties:
"Everyone is expected to be on constant MOOP patrol and leave every space better than they found it. General camp housekeeping is a shared responsibility. The maid is dead.
Campmates will also be expected to volunteer for scheduled shifts on XUZA during the week, helping with setup, breakdown, crew support, safety, and keeping the experience running smoothly for Black Rock City"
dues: We call camp dues the SIF, or Shared Infrastructure Fee. The 2026 SIF is $500 per person and goes directly toward the logistical and operational costs of camp infrastructure and running XUZA for the week, including fuel and propane for the flame effects.
cities: Las Vegas, Miami, New York
